summary: Full end-to-end testing of Vitruvian Construct (FastAPI + React + MongoDB). All 17 backend pytest cases pass (site payload, projects, concept-art with category filter, updates, subscribe with valid/duplicate/invalid email, admin auth + CRUD). Frontend home renders all sections; newsletter happy-path produces success state; concept-art category filter narrows grid to 1 character card, lightbox opens and closes via close button; /admin rejects wrong password with toast and unlocks Workshop Console with the 4 management sections on correct passphrase; creating a new transmission shows success toast and appears in the list.
critical_code_review_comments: Newsletter invalid-email path: type='email' + required means the native browser validator fires first; the JS regex/toast in Newsletter.jsx will rarely be reached. Functionally fine for users, but the requested 'invalid email shows error toast' is gated by HTML5 first. Consider adding novalidate or relying solely on the JS path if you want the custom toast to surface.

/api/subscribe returns 200 on duplicate (silently returning existing record). This matches the requested behavior, but consider returning 200 vs 201 distinction or an 'already_subscribed' flag in the payload for client UX.

Admin endpoints use a single shared password header — acceptable for an MVP admin, but ADMIN_PASSWORD defaults to a hardcoded value if env var missing. Document the requirement to set it in production .env.
All public list endpoints correctly project away mongo `_id` via projection — no ObjectId leakage observed.
seed_data_creation: Backend auto-seeded on startup (Irresponsible Axolotl project, 6 concept-art entries, 3 transmissions). Tests also created and removed TEST_-prefixed projects/art/updates; created a few TEST_-prefixed subscribers (left in DB intentionally — backend has no delete-subscriber endpoint).
context_for_next_testing_agent: backend_test.py covers all 17 critical API paths including admin auth gating. Re-run with: pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v. Frontend selectors verified working: newsletter-input/submit/success, archive-filter-{cat}, archive-card-{i}, archive-lightbox(-close), admin-password-input, admin-login-btn, admin-update-title/text/create. Subscribers list grows with each run — that is expected behaviour because subscribe is idempotent on email; use unique TEST_ emails per run.
